Add-on Treatment.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone, or abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone and enzalutamide (all added to standard androgen deprivation therapy)
Add-on Treatment.evidence: Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) ... to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial).
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatments are reported in the "Background" and "Methods" sections of the text.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: In the first 5 years of treatment, grade 3–5 toxic effects were higher when abiraterone was added to standard of care (271 [54%] of 498 vs 192 [38%] of 502 with standard of care)...

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: The document provides overall "grade 3-5 toxic effects" but does not specify the percentage or count of these that are *treatment-related* for Grade 3 or higher across all causes.
The document provides overall "grade 3-5 toxic effects" but does not specify the percentage or count of these that are *treatment-related* for Grade 3 or higher across all causes in the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Cardiac causes were the most common cause of death due to adverse events (five [1%] with standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide [two attributed to treatment]...
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Extract from Chunk 7 (page 11), "Cardiac causes were the most common cause of death due to adverse events (five [1%] with standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide [two attributed to treatment]...". The total number of patients in this arm is 445 (from the abstract). Value: 2 of 445.

The document mentions "one (<1%) with standard of care in the abiraterone trial" for cardiac deaths (Grade 5) in the control arm of the abiraterone trial, but it does not specify if this death was *treatment-related*. For the control arm of the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, Table 2 (page 10) reports 0 Grade 5 cardiac events, and no other Grade 5 events are specified as treatment-related.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: This column asks for an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT). This type of assessment is typically performed by external reviewers or in a meta-analysis of trials, rather than being reported within the primary publication of the trials themselves. The document describes the design, conduct, and results of the STAMPEDE trials but does not include an overall risk judgment or quality assessment of its own evidence criteria.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: Therefore, treatment efficacy with abiraterone is unlikely to be reduced by an increase in androgens or reactivation of ligand-binding domain-dependent reactivation of androgen receptor signalling (given that combining abiraterone with an effective androgen receptor antagonist, enzalutamide, has no discernible long-term benefit). (p. 453) Additionally, abiraterone acetate is known as an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor by its mechanism of action; this is clarified in the discussion to distinguish it from androgen receptor antagonists.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The "Discussion" section on page 11 states: "combining abiraterone with an effective androgen receptor antagonist, enzalutamide, has no discernible long-term benefit." This sentence distinguishes abiraterone from androgen receptor antagonists. Abiraterone acetate is known as an androgen biosynthesis inhibitor. Considering the first experimental arm (abiraterone trial), the class of agent is "Androgen biosynthesis inhibitor".

Control Arm.evidence: Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to either standard of care (control group) or standard of care plus abiraterone (in the abiraterone trial) or plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial). The standard of care was androgen deprivation therapy with luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists or antagonists or bilateral orchiectomy; after implementation of a protocol amendment (version 13; Dec 17, 2015), docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone (10 mg) orally once per day were permitted after starting androgen deprivation therapy but before starting study treatment.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The name of the control arm is "Standard of care". This is explicitly stated in the "Methods" section on page 3: "Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to either standard of care (control group) or standard of care plus abiraterone (in the abiraterone trial) or plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)." The components of the standard of care are described in the "Methods" section on page 3: "The standard of care was androgen deprivation therapy with luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists or antagonists or bilateral orchiectomy; after implementation of a protocol amendment (version 13; Dec 17, 2015), docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone (10 mg) orally once per day were permitted after starting androgen deprivation therapy but before starting study treatment."

Control Arm - N.evidence:
Between Nov 15, 2011, and Jan 17, 2014, 1003 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=502) or standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501) in the abiraterone trial.
- Figure 1 (Trial profile): "502 assigned to standard of care"
- Table 1: "Standard of care (n=502)"
Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: Extract from text on page 4, 5, 6. The value is mentioned in the 'Results' section on page 6, Table 1 on page 5, and Figure 1 on page 4.

The STAMPEDE platform protocol is also frequently referenced throughout the text.

Phase.evidence: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol (title); "We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol," (methods section)
Phase.plan_extraction_plan: The phase is mentioned in the title on page 1: "final results from two randomised phase 3 trials". It is also stated in the "Methods" section on page 3: "We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials, "Here, we aimed to evaluate long-term outcomes...", "The STAMPEDE abiraterone trial has now closed, 10 years after initiation of accrual, providing the opportunity to assess new data on long-term efficacy..." and "Here, we report long-term outcomes of patients in the abiraterone trial and primary results from the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial."
Original/Follow Up.plan_extraction_plan: The title on page 1 states "final results from two randomised phase 3 trials". The "Background" section on page 1 also mentions "Here, we aimed to evaluate long-term outcomes..." and "The STAMPEDE abiraterone trial has now closed, 10 years after initiation of accrual, providing the opportunity to assess new data on long-term efficacy of intensified hormone treatment...". The "Methods" section on page 3 states "Here, we report long-term outcomes of patients in the abiraterone trial and primary results from the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial."

Number of Arms Included.evidence: Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) ... to either standard of care (control group) or standard of care plus abiraterone (in the abiraterone trial) or plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial). Each trial randomly assigned patients to its own control group; there were no overlapping controls." ... "Figure 1: Trial profile... 502 assigned to standard of care ... 501 assigned to standard of care plus abiraterone ... 454 assigned to standard of care ... 462 assigned to standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide
Number of Arms Included.plan_extraction_plan: The "Methods" section on page 3 states: "We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol... Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) ... to either standard of care (control group) or standard of care plus abiraterone (in the abiraterone trial) or plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial). Each trial randomly assigned patients to its own control group; there were no overlapping controls." This indicates that there were two trials, and each trial had two distinct arms (one standard of care/control arm and one experimental arm). Since the control groups were non-overlapping, the total number of distinct arms across both trials is 2 + 2 = 4. This is further confirmed by Figure 1 (Trial profile) on page 4, which shows two arms for the abiraterone trial and two arms for the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: In terms of failure-free survival (HR 0·34 [0·29–0·39]; p<0·0001 vs HR 0·36 [0·31–0·43]; p<0·0001; figure 4C, D; appendix pp 5, 18). See Figure 4 legend and referenced curves: "Failure free survival (C, D)... HR 0·34 (95% CI 0·29–0·39); p<0·0001 [abiraterone trial] ... HR 0·36 (95% CI 0·31–0·43); p<0·0001 [abiraterone and enzalutamide trial]" (Page 451, Figure 4, curves C and D)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.page: 451 (and confirmed in figure description on page 9 per planner)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The hazard ratio (HR) with 95% confidence intervals (CI) for progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival) for the treatment arms compared to their respective control arms is reported. - For the abiraterone trial (Standard of care plus abiraterone vs Standard of care): HR 0.34 (95% CI 0.29–0.39); p<0.0001. - For the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial (Standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide vs Standard of care): HR 0.36 (95% CI 0.31–0.43); p<0.0001.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.plan_extraction_plan: A hazard ratio is a comparative measure of the treatment effect (treatment vs control). There is no separate hazard ratio reported *for* the control arm itself in isolation. The HRs provided describe the effect of the treatment arms relative to their respective control arms. Therefore, this specific metric (a hazard ratio *of* the control arm) is not reported. While survival curves for the control arm are shown in Figure 4C and 4D, specific PFS rates (percentages at time points) or median PFS times for the control arm are not explicitly stated in the text.

Region - N (%) | Europe | Treatment.evidence: Methods We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol, with no overlapping controls, conducted at 117 sites in the UK and Switzerland. Between Nov 15, 2011, and Jan 17, 2014, 1003 patients with metastatic prostate cancer were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=502) or standard of care plus abiraterone (n=501) in the abiraterone trial. Between July 29, 2014, and March 31, 2016, 916 patients were randomly assigned to standard of care (n=454) or standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide (n=462) in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial.
Region - N (%) | Europe | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan:

- Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial | Standard of care plus abiraterone and enzalutamide: 462 (100%)

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The protocol recruited patients with advanced prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy stratified by the presence or absence of distant metastases on conventional imaging (whole body bone scintigraphy or equivalent; CT or MRI of the pelvis, abdomen, and chest; and a chest x-ray if the chest was omitted) within 8 weeks of random assignment. Following a prespecified plan approved by the trial steering committee ... patients with non-metastatic and metastatic disease were separated for the reporting of long-term outcomes. ... Outcomes for patients with metastatic disease in the abiraterone trial after 5 years of follow-up ... and for those with non-metastatic disease ... have been published.14 Trial data for patients with metastatic disease in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial have not been reported previously. All patients classified as metastatic based on updated information at final database locking are included here.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: The study population is defined as patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy. This implies synchronous metastases, as patients were recruited based on the presence or absence of distant metastases at the start of treatment. This is stated in the "Methods" section on page 3, column 1: "The protocol recruited patients with advanced prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy stratified by the presence or absence of distant metastases on conventional imaging... within 8 weeks of random assignment." The entire reporting of outcomes is for this synchronously metastatic population.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: The PDF does not report results based on prognostic groups with metachronous metastases. The study focuses on patients with metastatic disease at the start of androgen deprivation therapy (synchronous).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence:
Physicians (AS, CJ, AH, and YJ) classified patients by high-volume or low-volume disease using the CHAARTED trial criteria2,15 based on the number and site of bone metastases and whether local sites stated at randomisation that visceral disease was present on CT scan.
AND
Given that metastatic volume is prognostic, outcomes split by low-volume and high-volume disease are of interest and were included as an additional prespecified subgroup analysis, in line with other trials of combination therapies in this setting.8,9,12
AND
Figure 3E on page 8 (450 in original, figure labeled "High-volume disease") provides overall survival analysis for high-volume metastatic disease subgroups.

Figure 3E on page 8 presents the overall survival subgroup analysis for high-volume disease.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Secondary outcomes were prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: Secondary outcomes were prostate cancer-specific survival (defined as the time from randomisation to death from prostate cancer); failure-free survival (defined as biochemical failure, local or lymph node progression, distant metastases, or death from prostate cancer); progression-free survival (defined as failure-free survival but excluding biochemical failure); metastatic progression-free survival (defined as progression of or new distant metastases or death from prostate cancer); symptomatic skeletal-related events (defined as pathological fracture or spinal cord compression or bone pain requiring radiotherapy or surgery); toxic effects and adverse events; and cost-effectiveness of research treatment.

Total Participants - N.plan_extraction_plan: The total number of participants is reported in the "Results" section on page 6, and visually represented in Figure 1 on page 4. The paper focuses on two trials for metastatic prostate cancer. The abiraterone trial included 1003 patients, and the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial included 916 patients. Summing these two groups gives the total participants for the two trials reported: 1003 + 916 = 1919.

The value is 501. For the "Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial", extract the number of patients in the "Standard of care plus abiraterone plus enzalutamide" arm from Figure 1: Trial profile on page 4. Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy) plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg orally once daily and prednisolone 5 mg orally once daily (in the abiraterone trial), administered until radiographical progression or another reason for discontinuation.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) using a computerised algorithm and a minimisation technique to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy ...), or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally... (Page 1) Abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) and prednisolone (5 mg; prednisone at Swiss sites) were given orally once a day in the abiraterone trial... (Page 3)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.plan_extraction_plan: Extract the description of the first experimental treatment arm from the "Background" section of the abstract on page 1, which details the regimen for the "abiraterone trial". This is further elaborated in the "Procedures" section on page 3.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally; abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) using a computerised algorithm and a minimisation technique to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy... allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial).
Treatment Arm(s).plan_extraction_plan: The names of the experimental treatment arms are reported in the "Background" and "Methods" sections of the text, and also as column headers in Table 1. Page 1: "standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally" and "abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)". Page 5-6: "Standard of care plus abiraterone" and "Standard of care plus enzalutamide and abiraterone".

Treatment Class.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (abiraterone) with or without enzalutamide, each added to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy with or without docetaxel)
Treatment Class.evidence: 1. “Background Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (herein referred to as abiraterone) or enzalutamide added at the start of androgen deprivation therapy improves outcomes for patients with metastatic prostate cancer. Here, we aimed to evaluate long-term outcomes and test whether combining enzalutamide with abiraterone and androgen deprivation therapy improves survival.” 2. “Methods We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol, with no overlapping controls... Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) using a computerised algorithm and a minimisation technique to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial).”
Treatment Class.plan_extraction_plan: The treatment classes are reported in the "Methods" section under "Randomisation and masking" on page 3, and also summarized in the "Background" section on page 1.

Trial.evidence: “Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol” (title) We analysed two open-label, randomised, controlled, phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol, with no overlapping controls… Patients were randomly assigned… to either standard of care… or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial).” (Methods) “In the abiraterone trial, median overall survival was 76·6 months… In the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, median overall survival was 73·1 months…” (Summary/Findings) Table 1 headline columns: “Abiraterone trial” and “Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial” (Page 449, Table 1, Page 450, Figure 3).
Trial.plan_extraction_plan: The document reports on two distinct randomized phase 3 trials conducted under the STAMPEDE platform protocol. These trials are consistently named throughout the paper. 'Abiraterone trial' is found in the title on page 1 (text), in the 'Background' section on page 1 (text), in the 'Methods' section on page 3 (text), and as a column header in Table 1 on pages 5 and 6, and as a figure title in Figure 2 (A, C, E) on page 7 and Figure 4 (A, C, E) on page 9. 'Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial' is found in the title on page 1 (text), in the 'Background' section on page 1 (text), in the 'Methods' section on page 3 (text), and as a column header in Table 1 on pages 5 and 6, and as a figure title in Figure 2 (B, D, F) on page 7 and Figure 4 (B, D, F) on page 9.

Type of Therapy.value: Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy (specifically, treatment arms: standard of care [androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed] or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg orally once per day, or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day)
Type of Therapy.evidence:
Title: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy: final results from two randomised phase 3 trials of the STAMPEDE platform protocol"
- "Patients were randomly assigned (1:1) using a computerised algorithm and a minimisation technique to either standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)."
Type of Therapy.plan_extraction_plan: The general description of the therapy is found in the title of the article on page 1 (Chunk 5): "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone with or without enzalutamide for patients with metastatic prostate cancer starting androgen deprivation therapy". More detailed descriptions of the specific treatment arms are provided in the "Methods" section on page 1 (Chunk 5): "standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy; docetaxel 75 mg/m² intravenously for six cycles with prednisolone 10 mg orally once per day allowed from Dec 17, 2015) or standard of care plus abiraterone acetate 1000 mg and prednisolone 5 mg (in the abiraterone trial) orally or abiraterone acetate and prednisolone plus enzalutamide 160 mg orally once a day (in the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial)."
